_purpose: Machine-readable preregistration plan for the Reality Audit public anisotropy program.
_note: Lock this file before any confirmatory run.  Do NOT modify after first confirmatory execution.

hypothesis.name: catalog_independent_sky_anisotropy
hypothesis.description: High-energy astrophysical event arrival directions show a statistically significant sky-position anisotropy that persists across independent instrument catalogs, after correcting for known instrument acceptance geometry.
hypothesis.null_hypothesis: Event arrival directions are consistent with isotropic (or acceptance-corrected-isotropic) sky coverage.  Any detected deviation is due to instrument bias, acceptance geometry, or random fluctuation.

null_model.note: Wide-field gamma-ray detectors use exposure_corrected null.  Neutrino detectors with approximately uniform RA coverage use isotropic null.

axis_scan.note: 192-axis grid (16 lon x 12 lat) for confirmatory runs; 48-axis grid acceptable for exploratory screening.

thresholds.note: Single-metric uncorrected percentile thresholds before multiple-testing correction.

multiple_testing_correction.note: Holm correction applied across the two primary statistics.  Secondary statistics are exploratory only and not used for discovery claims.

discovery_criteria.description: A discovery claim requires ALL of the following:
criteria: At least two independent catalogs each exceed exclusion_percentile individually
No known instrumental systematic explains the signal
Analysis was pre-registered and not modified after first confirmatory run

interpretation_guardrails: A statistically significant anisotropy is NOT evidence for any specific physical mechanism without independent theoretical motivation and Bayesian model comparison.
Small catalogs (N < 100) are statistically underpowered.  IceCube HESE has ~37 events; treat its results as exploratory regardless of percentile.
Instrument acceptance correction is an approximation; any surviving signal must be checked against detailed instrument-response Monte Carlo.
Do NOT report percentiles as p-values.  The empirical null distributes over 'null_repeats' samples; with 500 repeats, resolution floor is ~0.002.
Pre-trial significance does not account for researcher degrees of freedom; only results using EXACTLY this preregistered plan are confirmatory.

preregistration_metadata.note: Set registration_date before first confirmatory run.  Compute plan_hash_sha256 as sha256(json-canonical-form-of-this-file) to detect post-hoc modifications.
